title: incompatible with mysql in column-type-info
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
create table testfield (f0 int,f1 double,primary key (f0));
insert into testfield values (1,1.1);
insert into testfield values (2,2.2);

TiDB
[tidb@k8s-master tmp]$ mysql  --column-type-info -u root  -P 4000   -h [127.0.0.1](http://127.0.0.1/)  -A -p123123
mysql: [Warning] Using a password on the command line interface can be insecure.
Welcome to the MySQL monitor.  Commands end with ; or \g.
Your MySQL connection id is 585115960
Server version: 8.0.11-TiDB-v7.1.8-5.1 TiDB Server (Apache License 2.0) Enterprise Edition, MySQL 8.0 compatible

Oracle is a registered trademark of Oracle Corporation and/or its
affiliates. Other names may be trademarks of their respective
owners.

Type 'help;' or '\h' for help. Type '\c' to clear the current input statement.

mysql> use test
Database changedmysql> select cast(f1 as char),f1 from testfield;
Field   1:  `cast(f1 as char)`
Catalog:    `def`
Database:   ``
Table:      ``
Org_table:  ``
Type:       VAR_STRING
Collation:  utf8mb4_bin (46)
**Length:     0**
Max_length: 4
Decimals:   31
Flags:

Catalog:    `def`
Database:   `test`
Table:      `testfield`
Org_table:  `testfield`
Type:       NEWDECIMAL
Collation:  binary (63)
Length:     12
Max_length: 4
Decimals:   2
Flags:      NUM 

MySQL：

Field   1:  `cast(f1 as char)`
Catalog:    `def`
Database:   ``

| cast(f1 as char) | f1   |
+------------------+------+
| 1.10             | 1.10 |
| 2.20             | 2.20 |
+------------------+------+
2 rows in set (0.00 sec)

mysql>

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
Compatible with MySQL

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
incompatible with MySQL

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
v8.5.1

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
+------------------------+
| version()              |
+------------------------+
| 8.0.11-TiDB-v7.1.8-5.1 |
+------------------------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
poc: create table testfield (f0 int,f1 double,primary key (f0));
key_bug_features: incompatible with MySQL column type information
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE testfield2 (f0 INT, f1 FLOAT, f2 VARCHAR(10), PRIMARY KEY (f0));
SELECT f1, CAST(f1 AS CHAR) AS f1_char FROM testfield2 WHERE f1 > 1.0;
SELECT f2, CAST(NULL AS CHAR) AS null_cast FROM testfield2;
